findings.description: SQL injection (CWE-89): search_by_name interpolates the untrusted `fragment` argument into the query via f-string instead of a bound parameter, allowing filter bypass and UNION/boolean-blind exfiltration of other tables.

TOCTOU race / non-atomic check-then-act (CWE-367): reserve reads stock then UPDATEs in a separate statement, so concurrent callers can both pass the check and oversubscribe inventory below zero; the guard should be a conditional `UPDATE ... WHERE stock >= ?` checking rowcount in one transaction.

restock (and reserve) do not validate that `quantity` is non-negative, so a negative value silently inverts the stock operation; missing input validation with integrity impact (primarily a logic/validation concern, deferred to logic critic).
Off-by-one: `row["stock"] > quantity` rejects reservations when stock exactly equals quantity; should be `>=` (logic bug, deferred to logic critic).
`logger` is imported and configured but never used anywhere in the module (dead code).
